spec_match.rationale: Builds data: URLs both with and without `charset=` parameters and checks the fetched Response's content-type header — exactly the spec-conformance demonstration the Fetch spec section on data: URLs calls for.

interactive.rationale: Load-examples button renders two iframes side by side; a custom URL builder with MIME, charset, and content inputs runs an actual `fetch(url)` and reports content-type with PASS/FAIL coloring.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises constructing URLs with/without charset, iframe rendering verification, and a fetched content-type readout — all delivered.

edge_cases.rationale: Covers the headline (UTF-8 charset preservation) but doesn't demo the silent-corruption-with-ISO-8859-1 case the note describes, doesn't try invalid charsets (charset=Klingon), no base64 + charset combination, no multipart-style parameters.

design_system.rationale: Variables throughout, the inline `padding:0.4rem 0.85rem...` button uses the global button style which pairs background and color correctly, no static-card pattern.

openQuestions.title: Demo the ISO-8859-1 corruption case
openQuestions.detail: The note tells the visitor that pre-146 stripped charset would silently re-decode an ISO-8859-1 body as UTF-8. The iframe stage should ship an ISO-8859-1 example that visibly renders mojibake when the charset parameter is stripped, vs the correct accented characters when preserved.

openQuestions.title: Add a base64 + charset case
openQuestions.detail: Both `;base64` and `;charset=utf-8` are common together. Add an example URL that combines them so visitors see the encoding rules don't conflict.

openQuestions.title: Reject unknown charsets gracefully
openQuestions.detail: What happens when the data URL declares `charset=Klingon`? Show the browser's fallback so the visitor learns the failure mode, not just the happy path.

summary: Clean side-by-side iframe demo plus a working URL builder that exposes the fixed behaviour. To fully sell why the change matters, ship the ISO-8859-1 mojibake example the page note describes — that's the visible corruption mode the spec change actually prevents.
